summary: Compile-time binding generation for ReactiveUI bindings, observation APIs, trimming safety, and AOT-friendly code.
recommendedPatterns: Keep binding lambdas inline and simple so compile-time generation can optimize them.
Prefer compile-time bindings when AOT, trimming, or startup performance matters.
Use platform-specific binding packages where needed.
avoidPatterns: Do not hide binding expressions behind variables or indirection that breaks generator analysis.
Do not mix runtime and compile-time binding styles without team conventions.
setupSteps: Add ReactiveUI.Binding and any required platform package.
Watch diagnostics after refactors and renames.
commonPitfalls: Assuming classic runtime binding idioms will all translate to generator-optimized code.

harvest.sourceSummary: Curated from local ReactiveUI repositories, official GitHub documentation, and current SDK/package guidance.
harvest.notes: Prepared for the ReactiveUI MCP Server knowledge catalog.
